question.content: For the cantilever-beam and rigid-cylinder configuration in the accompanying image, beam AB has length l and bending stiffness EI, is fixed at A and free at B, and carries a downward concentrated force F at B. After loading, the segment near A stays in smooth contact with a rigid cylindrical surface of radius r, so the contact segment has constant curvature 1/r; the remaining segment is detached from the cylinder and deforms elastically. In this loading case, the tangent at A remains horizontal, and F > EI/(rl), so there is a boundary point C: segment AC is in contact with the cylinder while segment CB is free from contact. Use C as the connection between the contact segment and the free-bending segment, and compute the deflection at free end B. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content:
Let C be the separation point between contact and free-bending segments, and let x=BC. At C, the curvature equals the cylinder curvature:
\[ \frac{F x}{EI}=\frac{1}{r} \Rightarrow x=\frac{EI}{Fr}. \]
step_1.key_point: Determine point C first from curvature matching.

step_3.content:
Decompose free-end deflection into three parts:
\[ \Delta_B=\Delta_1+\Delta_2+\Delta_3. \]
1) Deflection of point C from contact geometry:
\[ (r-\Delta_1)^2=r^2-(l-x)^2 \Rightarrow \Delta_1=\frac{(l-x)^2}{2r}
=\frac{l^2}{2r}-\frac{EI}{Fr^2}+\frac{(EI)^2}{2F^2r^3}. \]
2) Additional B deflection due to rotation at section C:
\[ \Delta_2=\frac{l-x}{r}(l-x)=\frac{(l-x)^2}{r}
=\frac{l^2}{r}-\frac{2EI}{Fr^2}+\frac{(EI)^2}{F^2r^3}. \]
3) Bending deflection of free segment BC:
\[ \Delta_3=\frac{F x^3}{3EI}=\frac{(EI)^2}{3F^2r^3}. \]
step_3.key_point: Compute the three deflection components and sum them.

step_6.key_point: Final simplified expression of free-end deflection.
